sessions.name: session-registry
sessions.goal: Two-tier session registry for team visibility + agent skills + CLI tooling
sessions.scope: Session registry, merge logic, CLI commands, wizard, sidebar, skills. cc-tui v0.3.0.

sessions.name: fix-write-race
sessions.goal: cc-tui mutations lose state when chained with && in a single Bash call

sessions.name: session-scope-awareness
sessions.goal: Agents scan existing sessions before creating new ones, detect overlaps, update existing session scope when extending
sessions.scope: Add Cross-Session Teammate Awareness to the session-manager SKILL.md: Rust flags (--verbose, --cross) + SKILL.md protocol with 3 coordination patterns (Dependency, Redundancy, Related Work). In: CLI flags, SKILL.md protocol. Out: sequence support for --cross, NLP overlap detection, cross-workspace awareness.

sessions.name: scope-gate-protocol
sessions.goal: Agents flag out-of-scope requests and offer to create pending sessions or update current scope
sessions.scope: Insert a dedicated Scope-Gate Protocol section into session-manager SKILL.md between Team Awareness and Proactive Ideation Dashboard. Defines what triggers the gate (new feature, infra change, cross-cutting concern, different subsystem), what doesn't (clarifying questions, minor adjustments, explicit inclusions, quick one-liners), and a 3-step gate pattern: Flag → Offer (3 options: pending session, expand scope, quick aside) → Execute. Moves the comparison table from Proactive Ideation into this section since the gate is defined first. No Rust code changes.

sessions.name: proactive-ideation-dashboard
sessions.goal: Agents proactively offer to create pending sessions for ideas, tech debt, and improvements discovered during work
sessions.scope: During work, agents naturally discover tech debt, improvement ideas, missing features, and architectural smells. Instead of letting these evaporate, agents should acknowledge the session registry as an idea dashboard and proactively ask: 'I noticed X could be improved. It's out of scope for now but worth tracking. Want me to create a pending session?' This turns ccsm into a collective brain that accumulates institutional knowledge automatically.

sessions.name: ccsm-audit-and-vision
sessions.goal: -g Audit ccsm stability, identify gaps, and decide next-phase vision
sessions.scope: Stability audit of ccsm codebase + vision planning for next phase. Audit: test coverage, error handling, main.rs monolith risk, CLI surface consistency, registry edge cases, binary naming (cc-tui vs ccsm), session lifecycle bugs. Vision: what's the product? what's missing? what should v0.6/v1.0 look like? Output: structured assessment + prioritized backlog of sessions.

sessions.name: integration-tests
sessions.goal: Add integration tests to ccsm: CLI end-to-end, lifecycle, locking, edge cases
sessions.scope: Add integration tests to ccsm CLI. Use assert_cmd or std::process::Command to invoke the ccsm binary. Test: new→start→note→complete lifecycle, sequence batch operations, attach modes (auto-discover, --pid, UUID), rename with -g/-s, trash/recover, clean-all. Test locking: concurrent mutations don't race. Test edge cases: missing sessions, invalid names, duplicate detection. Target: ~15-20 integration tests that exercise the full CLI surface.

sessions.name: playbook
sessions.goal: Document common ccsm sequence chains and workflows as reusable recipes

sessions.name: Multiple claude session for one ccsm session
sessions.goal: Enable multiple claude session for a ccsm session to avoid chaos, large context window or produce some approaches
sessions.scope: Implement ccsm refresh: retire current Claude session to retired_session_ids list, spawn fresh claude (no --resume), inject CCSM_SESSION env var for agent self-awareness. New CLI subcommand: ccsm refresh <name> [--reason]. Registry schema adds retired_session_ids: [{id, retired_at, reason}]. One ccsm session → one active Claude session at a time, with history of retired ones.

sessions.name: unified-data-directory
sessions.goal: Refactor data directory from Claude-specific .claude/sessions to agent-neutral .ccsm/ layout supporting multiple agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
sessions.scope:
Current state: ccsm hardcodes .claude/sessions.json, ~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json, ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/. Goal: design and implement a unified data layout that works across agent platforms while maintaining backward compatibility with existing Claude Code sessions.

Key questions to resolve:
- New directory root? (.ccsm/ at workspace and ~/.ccsm/ at home)
- How to namespace per-agent sessions? (.ccsm/sessions/claude/, .ccsm/sessions/codex/, etc.)
- Transcript format differs by agent — do we care or just store them?
- Migration path for existing .claude/ data
- Hook compatibility — do other agents have hook equivalents?

Approach: read current code paths, enumerate all .claude/ references, design the new layout, implement a configurable root with claude as default, add migration subcommand.

sessions.name: persisted-session-details
sessions.goal: Decide whether to persist ccsm session details on GitHub per-project, answered via 5W1H model
sessions.scope:
Current state: session detail files live in .claude/sessions/*.md per workspace — local only, not shared. The question: should these be committed and pushed to GitHub per project? Evaluate using 5W1H framework:

**What** — what artifacts would be persisted? (sessions.json, detail .md files, retired session history)
**Why** — team visibility, cross-machine resume, audit trail, backup. Tradeoff: clutter in git log, potential merge conflicts on sessions.json
**When** — on every session lifecycle event? only at completion? at session start?
**Who** — who sees/uses persisted details? teammates, future self, CI systems?
**Where** — same repo? separate tracking repo? ccsm-specific branch?
**How** — auto-commit from hooks? manual via agent? ccsm subcommand?

Expected output: clear recommendation with rationale, edge cases flagged, no code changes (decision doc only).

sessions.name: CCSM session acknowlege
sessions.goal: Evaluate agents's acknowledge about their CCSM session to improve inject-scope
